name: Innocence Project
mission: By entering your email address, you agree to receive emails from the Innocence Project. By entering your phone number, you agree to receive recurring automated promotional and personalized marketing text messages (e.g. cart reminders) from The Innocence Project at the cell number used when signing up. Consent is not a condition of any purchase. Reply HELP for help and STOP to cancel. Msg frequency varies. Msg & data rates may apply. View Terms & Privacy.
theoryOfChange: By using DNA evidence to prove innocence in individual cases, the organization identifies systemic failures (like eyewitness misidentification or false confessions) and uses that data to advocate for legislative and judicial reforms to prevent future wrongful convictions.
scoreSummary: The Innocence Project Inc shows moderate impact and limited alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Research published on death investigations and forensic methods
Studies on racial and gender bias in the legal system

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 19/50, the charity is categorized as a Sadaqah-eligible advocacy organization rather than a direct relief provider [7]. It offers a strong track record for donors interested in justice reform, though it may have lower fit for those prioritizing immediate humanitarian aid [1][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on systemic change through litigation and policy reform, reporting a total revenue of $33,643,969 to support these efforts [1][2]. While it demonstrates effectiveness in securing exonerations, the impact score of 23/50 reflects a high cost per beneficiary of $86,746.42 and a program expense ratio of 74.6% [1][4][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Innocence Project Inc works to exonerate the wrongly convicted and reform the criminal justice system through DNA testing and policy advocacy.
strengths: Exceptional financial health with 41.7 months of working capital available
High fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.10 to raise every $1
Strong track record of achieving full exonerations for the wrongly convicted
baseline_narrative.summary: The Innocence Project Inc is a national nonprofit dedicated to freeing innocent individuals and preventing future wrongful convictions through post-conviction litigation and systemic policy reform [2][4]. The organization maintains a perfect Charity Navigator score of 100.0/100, reflecting its commitment to transparency and financial health [1].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial accountability and systemic impact, balanced by a high cost per beneficiary and advocacy-focused mission.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic criminal justice reform and value organizations with impeccable transparency and long-term financial sustainability.

Value a perfect 100/100 rating for accountability and transparency
Prefer investing in established institutions with a 30-year track record
Are comfortable with a high-cost, high-impact systemic change model

Impact is measured in legal victories and policy shifts rather than volume of beneficiaries

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A reflects verified exoneration counts and a documented theory of change with high transparency.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization uses DNA evidence to prove innocence, leveraging these cases to advocate for policy reforms that prevent future wrongful convictions.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In legal advocacy, verified exonerations serve as the gold standard for proving the effectiveness of litigation strategies.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the organization has high reserves, the scale of wrongful convictions in the US provides significant room for expanded litigation and policy work.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">100/100 score for Accountability & Finance</cite> from Charity Navigator, reflecting rigorous governance and disclosure practices.
The charity holds <cite id="1">41.7 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing immense long-term stability for multi-year legal battles.
Since its inception, the project has secured <cite id="4">hundreds of full exonerations</cite>, directly freeing innocent individuals from incarceration.

strengths_deep_dive: The Innocence Project's strength lies in its dual-track impact model. On one hand, it provides direct legal services that have led to <cite id="5">220 exonerations</cite> by 2019. On the other, it leverages these cases to drive systemic change. Their financial health is a significant asset; with <cite id="2">$105,843,645 in total assets</cite>, they have the staying power required for the criminal justice reform space. Furthermore, their <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> ensures objective oversight of their <cite id="2">$32,455,846 annual budget</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Innocence Project Inc is a national nonprofit organization <cite id="1">founded in 1992</cite> with a mission to exonerate the wrongly convicted through DNA testing and reform the criminal justice system. The organization has achieved <cite id="4">at least 220 total exonerations</cite> as of 2019, utilizing a multi-pronged approach that includes post-conviction litigation, policy reform, and strategic litigation. Financially, the organization is exceptionally stable, reporting <cite id="2">$32,455,846 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their operations are characterized by high efficiency in resource acquisition, spending only <cite id="1">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond direct legal representation, the Innocence Project engages in massive administrative review, having processed <cite id="5">1,806 intake letters</cite> in a single year to identify potential cases of wrongful conviction. While the cost per beneficiary is high due to the intensive nature of legal advocacy, the organization maintains a <cite id="1">perfect overall score of 100/100</cite> from Charity Navigator, signaling top-tier accountability and leadership standards.
